title: Doing Justice To Human Life | HG Amitasana Dasa | SB 4. 4 .4 | 2019-07-26 |
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Sati was going very fast so that she might not be checked by husband but she
was immediately followed by many thousands of disciples of Lord Shiva
headed by the Yakshas, Maniman and Mada. The word Gata Vyataha used in this
connection means without fear. Sati did not care that she was going alone
therefore she was almost fearless. The word Anu Charaha is also
significant for it indicates that Lord Shiva's disciples were always ready to
sacrifice anything for Lord Shiva. All of them could understand the desire of
Shiva who did not want Sati to go alone. Anu Charaha means those who can
immediately understand the purpose of their master. Thus ends the Vaktivedanta.
visited someone's house, had gone to meet someone and this morning time
and I was received well in the house and they asked me to please sit down and it
took about 15 minutes for him to come as I was sitting in the drawing room and
young son also sitting and watching the television and in the television he was
watching something on National Geography that was the channel which was playing
and since I was waiting I also happened to see what was coming although haven't
watched any channel for many years now so just curious as to what what the Sun
is watching so what about 15 minutes and it was a huge LED big TV so very crystal
clear very nice photography I mean technology has advanced I remember when
I was young there was initially black and white television small 14 inch
and then after that first time color television came so compared to that
these days things are quote-unquote advanced we are living in advanced world
so this episode was focusing on the jungle cat there was a cat jungle cat
wild cat and it was all about 15 minutes
I was just you know also keeping what a scripture say in mind from Shastra
perspective so 15 minutes it was all about how this cat goes about making
arrangements for its eating not only for its eating feeding the young ones how it
is making arrangement to defend in the daytime defend in the night time defend
at the time when there are dangers from other wild animals
then how it is sleeping in the night and while it is sleeping how it is making
arrangements for protection and defense because their animals in the night time
also can attack and no doubt procreation so this 15 minutes had all these four
things very nicely graphically displayed or you know projected
and if you ask any animal if you take morning to night all what they are doing
are Shastra's point or broadly their actions can be classified in these four
categories which is to do with eating sleeping procreation and defense and I
could see that all though it was very nicely shown there was a lot of�
shot all the activities of this wildcat was broadly classified in these four
categories only there was nothing else which it can do our scriptures point out
that human form of life like here it is mentioned in the purpose those who can
immediately understand the purpose of their master so this world also has been
created by the Lord and there is a purpose the Lord has created anything we
see created anything wonderful anything orderly anything which is done
systematically it is done for a purpose it is not done just for the heck of
doing things or done whimsically with no purpose with no intention with no goal
so the Lord has created this world for a purpose
and unfortunately especially in Kali Yuga most of the people are ignorant of
the purpose of this creation why we are in this material world what is the
purpose of human form of life what is the ultimate goal what does the Lord
expect us to do .... and ignorant of the purpose of the
creation or the purpose for which the Lord has sent us here the purpose for
what we have come here and we have got this human body, we are acting in ways
which are detrimental to our self-interest. Just like here Sati,
although Lord Shiva forbade her not to go but without knowing the understanding
the purpose of the master, purpose of that if she goes what's the danger
awaiting her, she happily tread that path. So it is very important that here
it is mentioned Anucha Raha which means that the subordinates of Lord Shiva
understood the purpose of the master. Why is he refusing Sati, why is he asking
Sati not to go? So this human form of life
we all are it is said that labdhava sudurlabham bahusambhavanti after many
many lives of transmigration in different life forms we get this human
birth and in this human birth we have got developed intelligence to
understand the purpose of our creation, purpose of this world, who is God.
In animal forms they can't understand, they don't have that ability, they don't
have that so-called intelligence to understand who we are, what's the purpose
of our creation, what happens after death. and in we see today's world it's
unfortunate because of ignorance because of not having the understanding of higher

So scriptures point out that if we also,
use our developed intelligence which can enable us to understand the higher purpose,
the purpose of this creation, about God, our relationship with Him. If we do not use that
intelligence for that purpose, then we are at best, just like we have small thief and
a big thief, we are refined, sophisticated, well dressed, two legged animals.
Dvipada pashu. It looks very harsh but it's a fact. If we use only that portion of intelligence
which enables us to eat better, sleep better, defend better, mate better, then we are just
animals but little more refined, more sophisticated.
Prabhupada was very blunt, very straight forward in his talks, he said that animal may have
Openly, but a man will have in a very very nice air conditioned multistoried building,
very with nice incense sticks burning, very very serene, very very well decorated, this
thing. But the act is the same. The pleasure is the same. Animals may defend using claws
and dogs may defend barking. But we may defend using intercontinental ballistic missiles.
all this thing is about you know uranium enrichment, atom bombs and you know all
these kinds of missiles are getting tested and all the nations are becoming
very very fearful. It's all about defend. They may do in one way, we are doing in a
very very technologically advanced sophisticated ways but the act is the
same. So our scriptures point out that if we don't understand the purpose, if we
don't seek higher level of understanding, consciousness, purification
of consciousness then we are spoiling, we are wasting this wonderful opportunity
which we have got in this human birth. And such a person who is spoiling this
opportunity, Bhagavad Gita Prabhupada uses the word in the purport Kripana.
Kripana means miser. Who is a miser?
Miser, in Hindi they call conjus. Who is a miser, conjus? I had an experience when I was young of a family
staying just two houses away from our house. And I still remember although I
was just about five or six years old that time. So this person he had three, two
otter and when we would go to their home for generally when guests come somebody
comes children come you know you give them something to eat so he will sit in
a chair in the center and then three children are there and we will be there
and he will have that Chali you know Chali Bhutta that corn in his hand he'll
remove three pieces and give it to one three pieces and give to another so in
other words it was very very I mean there many other reasons to say that he
was miser somebody may be thinking that he must be doing out of hospitality or
love it was because he didn't wanted to let go what he had in his hand just okay
tin dana below and he had a car in those days it was not an antique car but was
antique because in spite of he having the means to buy the car
you know he had a car in those days it was not an antique car but was antique because in spite of he having the means to buy the car
he would have a car which actually does not move and once in a while when he
would want to move for some reason we all would be called in the neighborhood
and you know typically old cars if you remember someone they don't especially
in winter season you have to push and then they'll put the gear and suddenly
it starts working so we have done that so he was like that so who is a miser
miser is a person who has the wealth but he doesn't have the means to buy the car
but the wealth is locked up and he eats like a beggar stays like a beggar he
doesn't want to spend the wealth he has the means to live decent life but he's
because of his miserly nature he doesn't want to spend a lot of people living
like a beggar beggar is living like a beggar because he does not have the
means he does not have the wealth therefore is living like a beggar
you know having tattered clothes he cannot afford cannot buy new clothes but
imagine a person who has the means has the wealth but still he has clothes
which are all tattered living in a very shabby condition money is locked up in
the lockers he is called a miser our scriptures say that if in the human form
we use only that part of the intelligence which enables us to
facilitate eating sleeping mating and defending
and we also are misers so here it is said anu charaha means those who can
immediately understand the purpose of their master so here Maniman Mada and the
followers the bull Nandi they could understand the purpose of their master
and hence when Sati is proceeding they are going behind her so even as devotees
even as exalted personalities why do they go behind her when Sati is going on her way?
why do they go behind her when Sati is going on her way?
people. Sometimes people feel that mere piche pad gayo. Even preachers sometimes
you make calls, please come for the session. A person may feel that you know
why are they after me? Because pure devotees, acharyas, they understand the
purpose of their master. They understand the purpose of this creation. They
understand that if we are acting recklessly or whimsically without
understanding the purpose in ignorance, we'll be miserable. We'll suffer more. And
it's a fact in today's world, although we are living in a technological world, more
and more people are in anxiety. More and more people are worried. More and more
people are committing suicides. I was reading one article which
says that suicide action is brave but act is cowardly. First time I heard
action is brave for someone to go to 30th floor and then jump. It's a very
brave act. Brave action. Jump. But act is cowardly. Recently in Mumbai one person
From a multi-storied building and died. And he was very wealthy, well-to-do,
accomplished. Son, daughter both are in the US. He was in a good position. Just
jumped from 30th floor. Told his wife that I'm going out and two hours, three
hours. Didn't run. They became and the son daughter had come from the US. So they
became little curious as to what happened.
They entered to the house. He was sitting by the balcony and in the bedroom.
They started looking for them. And suddenly they found the slippers at the end
of the balcony. Generally when a person moves out. And when they looked out of the balcony
he had only jumped, so people had gathered around that body. They immediately rushed
So this lady was telling me. This lady who attends our session here, who was the first
session and is known to this person, family friend. He says he was very nice. He also
was quite bubbly, quite you know, social person. But last 3-4 months he was quite, quite very,
some kind of a depression. So then I tried, I didn't get too much into details, I tried
understanding what went wrong, why did it happen. So the reason is, as scriptures, Garud
Puran says that when a person, at the point of death, death is very painful. They say
that the point of death, the pain which a person experiences is equivalent to the pain
of biting, simultaneous biting of forty-two thousand scorpions. Just like, the body of
Bhagavad Gita gives the size of the soul.
Just to give you a sense as to what is the size.
One ten thousand part of the tip of the ear.
So it says what is the, we don't, we remember the experience of the pain which a person will experience at the point of death.
So Garud Paran says the pain which a person will experience is equivalent to simultaneous biting of 42,000 scorpions.
And it seems that scorpion bite, we don't have an experience.
So we don't have an experience.
It seems that when a scorpion bites, people die not because of that poison in the bite.
They die because of the pain because of that bite.
Now the Garud Paran says, why, why, now for us to understand how can it be so painful.
Just about three months back I was coming from Mumbai.
And I was working on a laptop in the plane.
Unfortunately, before leaving, you know, when they say please put your seats upright.
Tighten your seat belts and all that.
I kept the laptop in that seat in front.
And in a hurry, I just came out of the aircraft, came to the, this thing, and left the laptop behind.
Somebody else also was there with me.
And then we came, we checked, we removed our bag, checked in bag, all that we removed.
And suddenly I said my handbag is very light.
Generally, you know, you have some sense if laptop is there and few other things.
It has to be of some weight.
I found this, it's very light.
I opened the zip and found the laptop is not there.
Believe me, although we know so much knowledge, we should be poised in happiness and distress.
And I put a real bad show in front of the person who was accompanying.
All that Krishna consciousness was gone.
And that person, Prabhuji, ho jayega, ho jayega.
Prabhuji, aap chinta mat karo, mil jayega.
He is telling me, chinta mat karo aap.
And then we went to that Indigo, the counter where lost baggage is there, immediately.
I said, please, I have that laptop.
So fortunately, you know, they have walkie-talkies and before the aircraft gets ready for the next flight.
So they, the people go in and.
They clean up the aircraft and all that.
So fortunately, they also had walkie-talkies.
So immediately, they know that people leave items like that.
So they have some mechanism to sort out these things.
So he immediately called on walkie-talkie, the person who is in charge of that aircraft.
And he said, this one laptop.
And along with me, two, three other also people were also there with similar.
So one level of chinta reduced because I am not alone.
When you go to a doctor with a disease, he said, allon ko bhi hota hai na?
Your pain increases if you know that you are the only one who is suffering.
And then he said, he called on walkie-talkie.
And then he said, aa mil gaya, ek laptop pada hai.
Then he said, aata hai abhi.
It took about ten minutes and fortunately, I got the laptop.
It was so painful because I didn't have taken a backup.
I had not taken a backup for few months.
And lot of things were there.
Imagine last two, three months of work which you have done.
You know, you worked on excel.
You worked on so many documents.
It's almost like losing three, four months of your work.
So then I immediately came back and looked for a backup solution.
Imagine one laptop is taken away from you.
It's so painful because you are all what you did three months is going down the drain.
At the point of death, you lose your laptop also, your desktop, your mobile phone, your father, your mother, your relatives, all your degrees.
All what you have so painstakingly 30 years, 40 years, 50 years you have worked and accumulated.
You don't want it to go but gone.
Imagine I could experience a sense of what it is.
Three months of work is going down the drain.
You can always buy another laptop.
It's not the cost of the laptop.
We went for cremation and all that.
So our scriptures point out that this death is inevitable.
In fact they say as sure as death.
One who is taken birth is sure to die.
One who dies is sure to take birth again.
So therefore scriptures say this human form of life we've got this developed intelligence.
Now that we have got this human birth we can inquire.
We can understand these higher things.
If we dabble in only the things which animals are dabbling in.
In a more sophisticated refined way.
Then we miss the main point.
Human life is meant for inquiry.

bliss. Now we also habitually may end up doing something wrong. We also are not in
that sense pure devotees. We also make mistakes. We also get sometimes attracted
to the glitter of this material world. We also are prone to sense gratification. We
also indulge in sense gratification at times. But immediately after this, because
of this higher knowledge, because of this understanding, there's an element of
guilt. There's an element of I shouldn't have done it. The other day somebody was
asking a question, planning, determined planning to avoid sense gratification.
Yes, we will fall because you know just like a child who is learning to walk
cannot learn to run and walk in one day. But every time we fall, we should learn
from the mistake. Pray to the Lord.
Make plans again and then rise up and again walk. A time will come when we'll
stop falling or the frequency at which we'll fall will reduce. All of a sudden
we cannot stop. Because that sense gratification, that desire for sense
gratification, material desires is over many many lifetimes we have cultivated. In
present life we are cultivating spiritual desires. But over many many lifetimes the
common phenomenon is indulge. Indulge. Because in all the millions of life
forms we have taken, the common understanding in all the life forms is
I'll be happy if I indulge in my senses. In that sense, sometimes we may feel
little depressed that I've been chanting for three years, five years, eight years.
Still the desires are there. Because compared to many lifetimes is 15 years,
20 years is nothing. So because we have been doing that, it has become a very
habitual response. Before we can even think, we start acting instinctively at
times. Animals act instinctively. So we also have some instinctive behavior
because of past conditioning. And if we are very diligent in taking the medicine,
following the process, hearing from Nityam Bhagavata Sevaya, understanding the
purpose of the creation, understanding the purpose of the Master, have these
instructions readily available with us, then and we pray to the Lord. We are
humble enough. We are not very proud of our knowledge. Sometimes we can be very
proud. Now I know. Many times when I travel, there was a time when I used to
be confident. Tuck, tuck, tuck. These five, six instructions were there in my pocket.
Anytime Maya will come, tuck, these instructions I'll apply. So I realized
Maya, you know, has many traps, gross and subtle. Even before you pull out,
everything is over. So then I realized, you know ultimately, you have to be
humble. Mama Maya Dhuratte Aya. Your formulas will not work. Although you can try as a
matter of duty to express to the Lord, that Lord, you don't, I don't want this.
Krishna wants us to surrender to Him. That's the biggest mistake we
have done. Exerted our independence from Him, our own plans
for our happiness and Krishna has created this wonderful world. Maya is
there which is battering us, will not get relief unless we come to our senses and
surrender to Him. It's a beautiful world in that sense. It's a beautiful Lord who
has created this world to bring us back to our senses. So nice. Stop here.
